summary: Implemented the non-destructive DB reset plan to get the app running. Root cause of recurring schema errors: the user's SWP_Nhom3 DB was created from swp_nhom3.sql with a schema incompatible with the JPA entities/DataSeeder. Created a fresh empty database SWP_Nhom3_App (leaving the old DB intact to preserve data), pointed spring.datasource.url to it in application.properties, and started the backend with JAVA_HOME=JDK25. DataSeeder built the correct schema and seeded 6 demo users + 20 products with no errors. Backend started on 8096; frontend (Next.js) started on 3000 and returns 200; backend /api/products returns APPROVED products. Earlier code fixes remain: product status ACTIVE->APPROVED, ensureColumn bracket-strip, ports backend 8096/frontend 3000.
current_status: App fully running: backend http://localhost:8096 (DB SWP_Nhom3_App), frontend http://localhost:3000. Demo login admin@example.com/password (also staff/user/seller/alice/bob @example.com). Old SWP_Nhom3 DB untouched. Both servers running in background terminals.
